rules.name: Base Unit System
rules.description: Use multiples of a base unit (4px or 8px) for all spacing values.
rules.application: Padding, margins, and gaps should be 4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64px.
rules.example: Card padding of 24px, gap between cards of 16px, section margin of 64px.
rules.anti_pattern: Using arbitrary spacing like 13px, 17px, 22px with no system.

rules.name: Density Levels
rules.description: Define compact, default, and comfortable density variants.
rules.application: Use compact density for data-heavy views, comfortable for marketing.
rules.anti_pattern: Using the same loose spacing for both data tables and marketing pages.

rules.name: Component Internal Spacing
rules.description: Maintain consistent padding within components of the same type.
rules.application: All buttons use the same vertical and horizontal padding ratio.
rules.example: Buttons with 12px vertical, 24px horizontal padding consistently.
rules.anti_pattern: Some buttons with 8px/16px padding, others with 14px/20px.

rules.name: Section Separation
rules.description: Use larger spacing to separate major content sections.
rules.application: Section gaps should be 2-4x the intra-section spacing.
rules.example: Items within a section at 16px gap, sections separated by 64px.
rules.anti_pattern: Equal 16px spacing everywhere, making sections blend together.

rules.name: Touch Target Sizes
rules.description: Interactive elements must be at least 44x44px for touch devices.
rules.application: Buttons, links, and controls should meet minimum touch target size.
rules.example: Icon buttons with 44px minimum touch area even if icon is 24px.
rules.anti_pattern: Small 24px icon buttons that are difficult to tap on mobile.

rules.name: Responsive Spacing
rules.description: Reduce spacing proportionally on smaller viewports.
rules.application: Desktop padding of 64px can become 32px on tablet, 16px on mobile.
rules.example: Hero section padding: 96px desktop, 48px tablet, 24px mobile.
rules.anti_pattern: Maintaining 96px padding on mobile, wasting precious screen real estate.
